about.title: About
about.subtitle: Scientist, entrepreneur and professor bridging basic research and applied medicine.
about.metaDesc: Full Professor at San Raffaele University, Rome, and founder and director of the Human Longevity Program at IRCCS San Raffaele. A career spanning Pisa, Houston and Rome.

bio.title: Biography
bio.p1: Born in Latina, Italy, on August 20, 1977, Ennio Tasciotti is an international scientist and pioneer in the fields of biomimetic medicine, nanotechnology, and regenerative medicine.
bio.p2: After excelling in Italy, he spent 15 years in the United States climbing the ranks of biomedical research at the Houston Methodist Hospital (Texas), where he founded and directed cutting-edge research centers.
bio.p3: He is currently the Director of the Human Longevity Program at the IRCCS San Raffaele in Rome, merging molecular biology, engineering, and medicine to develop new translational therapies and healthy aging protocols.

about.bioIntro: Born in Latina, Italy, on August 20, 1977, Ennio Tasciotti is an international scientist and pioneer in the fields of biomimetic medicine, nanotechnology, and regenerative medicine.

science.title: Science
science.subtitle: Nanomedicine, biomimetic materials and longevity medicine — three research areas, 200+ publications, 15 international patents.
science.metaDesc: Nanomedicine, biomimetic materials, longevity medicine: three research areas, 200+ publications, 15 international patents.
science.heroManifesto: My research has merged engineering, biology and medicine to overcome biological barriers in cancer, inflammatory disease and tissue regeneration.

nano.title: Nanomedicine
nano.body: Nanoscale delivery systems engineered to bypass biological barriers and deliver therapeutics with precision into diseased tissue. The Multistage Vector (Nature Nanotechnology, 2008) and the Leukosome (Nature Materials, 2016) are among the most cited outcomes.

regen.body: Extracellular matrix scaffolds and engineered materials that guide bone, cartilage and cardiac tissue regeneration by modulating local immune response without toxicity.

macroAreas.title: Drug Delivery
macroAreas.subtitle: Nanomedicine and Targeted Transport
macroAreas.body: Prof. Tasciotti has conducted extensive research into the utilization of micro-, nano-, and bio-materials for drug delivery purposes. By integrating nanotechnology with cell biology, he developed medical tools that operate in harmony with natural laws. His focus has been on designing implantable and injectable platforms to surmount biological barriers, target inflammation, administer drugs for cancer treatment, and modulate immune responses. The creation of the Multi Stage Vector, featured as the cover story in Nature Nanotechnology in 2008, marked the inception of targeted chemotherapy nanosystems and was recognized as one of the 'Five big ideas for nanotechnology' in Nature Medicine in 2009. He introduced the use of biomimicry principles to engineer bioinspired systems that evade reticulo-endothelial clearance, target inflamed vessels, traverse endothelial layers, and enhance therapeutic accumulation in the cellular cytoplasm. Noteworthy contributions include the Leuko-Like Vector, porous nanoneedles, and exosome-like vesicles.

macroAreas.title: Regenerative Medicine and Tissue Engineering
macroAreas.subtitle: Biomimetic Scaffolds and Extracellular Matrix
macroAreas.body: Prof. Tasciotti has contributed to the understanding of the healing potential inherent within the human body, endeavoring to maximize it by engineering biomimetic scaffolds capable of eliciting targeted cell and tissue responses. These materials are meticulously crafted to emulate the natural tissue response observed during physiological healing processes. His investigations demonstrated that both immune and stem cells exhibit distinct responses to implanted materials based on their composition, structure, and surface characteristics. His pioneering work in tissue engineering led to the development of scaffolds and membranes that closely mimic native tissue at both nano- and micro-scales, imbuing synthetic constructs with the functional properties of natural tissues. His primary focus lies in the advancement of translational biomaterials for musculoskeletal tissue engineering, mimicking the extracellular matrix of multiple tissues to facilitate superior regeneration of composite tissues.

publications.title: Evaluation of the osteoinductive potential of a bio-inspired scaffold mimicking the osteogenic niche for bone augmentation.
Chondroitin sulfate immobilized on a biomimetic scaffold modulates inflammation while driving chondrogenesis.

Multiscale patterning of a biomimetic scaffold integrated with composite microspheres.

macroAreas.title: Immune and Stem Cell Biology
macroAreas.subtitle: Immunomodulation
macroAreas.body: He made significant contributions to the understanding of stem cell and immune cell responses to biomaterials. His research has shown that it is feasible to influence cell fate within the body directly, eliminating the need for ex vivo cell expansion or manipulation. His team was among the pioneers in developing technologies that harness the activity of immune cells as initial responders following injury, aiming to recruit stem cells and facilitate neoangiogenesis. His approach to biomaterials engineering centers on regulating the response of inflammatory cells to the implant. By deciphering the cascade of inflammatory events, his research has shown that activation of M1 macrophages can lead to detrimental immune dysfunction and fibrosis. Conversely, M2 switching is associated with accelerated tissue healing and can prevent scar tissue formation. To enhance implant biocompatibility, his team has developed several immune-instructive biomaterials capable of modulating the immune response to promote improved tissue regeneration.

publications.title: Rapamycin-loaded biomimetic nanoparticles reverse vascular inflammation.

macroAreas.title: Translational Research and Clinical Applications
macroAreas.subtitle: Clinical Imaging and Regenerative Matrices
macroAreas.body: Expanding the scope of biomimetic approaches to unresolved clinical challenges, Prof. Tasciotti initiated a program focusing on the utilization of platelet-rich plasma (PRP) as a biomaterial in regenerative medicine. Investigations revealed that this blood-derived product substantially enhances early neovascularization and tissue deposition, while also modulating immune responses and mitigating host degradation of biologic matrices. The research explored innovative regenerative matrices, including repurposed lung tissue, which, following a decellularization process, offers architectural and biochemical cues to native cells. Additionally, he played a pivotal role in the advancement of novel imaging techniques, such as ultrasound elastography, to elucidate the progressive mechanisms involved in both hard and soft tissue healing in vivo.

publications.title: Loss of p53 drives neuron reprogramming in head and neck cancer.

ventures.subtitle: Turning world-class research into market value and technological innovation.

vision.title: Entrepreneurial Vision and Technology Transfer
vision.body1: Prof. Ennio Tasciotti combines a rigorous academic background with a proven entrepreneurial vision, translating complex biological observations into scalable engineering solutions. His translational research has direct clinical applicability, supported by 12 international patents covering nanomaterials and biomaterials.
vision.body2: Among his most prominent industrial projects is the 'Fracture Putty' program, funded by the DARPA (Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency) through multiple grants totaling approximately $15 million, aimed at developing bionanotechnological scaffolds for traumatic bone regeneration.
vision.body3: Furthermore, he championed the 'democratization of nanotechnology' by openly publishing standardized protocols for manufacturing biomimetic nanoparticles via microfluidics, making the technology accessible and affordable to laboratories worldwide.

ventures.startupsIntro: Active in the biotechnology ecosystem, he sits on the boards of directors of several companies where he holds top roles in scientific direction (R&D).

longevity.title: The Science of Longevity
longevity.subtitle: Engineering Healthspan to compress morbidity and optimize functional health.
longevity.metaDesc: Engineering Healthspan to compress morbidity and optimize functional health through longevity medicine.
longevity.introText1: The global demographic landscape is undergoing an irreversible structural mutation: epidemiological projections indicate that by 2100 the over-65 demographic will triple, representing 24% of the world population. However, the extension of chronological life has generated a clinical paradox. There is currently a systemic gap of 12.7 years between lifespan (total life duration) and healthspan (years lived in full functional health, autonomy, and independence).
longevity.introText2: The economic and caregiving burden of this discrepancy weighs heavily on welfare systems: 80% of all current medical costs are directly attributable to only four modifiable behavioral and environmental determinants, defined as the "4Ss" (Sedentary lifestyle, dietary Choices, chronic Stress, excesses). Modern longevity medicine does not pursue the mere temporal extension of old age, but aims at the protection and optimization of cellular and systemic biological resilience to align health expectancy with life expectancy.
longevity.stat1Lbl: The average gap to be bridged between chronological life expectancy and functional health.
longevity.stat2Lbl: Of global healthcare spending generated by modifiable lifestyle factors.
longevity.stat3Lbl: The clinical paradigm applied by the Human Longevity Program at IRCCS San Raffaele: a Predictive, Preventive, Personalized, and Participatory science.
longevity.social1Text: The 2026 epidemiological data are clear: we have extended lifespan, but biological quality lags behind by nearly 13 years. 80% of the clinical burden and global healthcare costs are driven by modifiable behavioral factors. Longevity medicine is not cosmetic anti-aging; it is the engineering of cellular resilience to compress morbidity and preserve autonomy. Here are the numbers driving our research. 📊
longevity.pillarsSubtitle: Regulating the molecular and cellular mechanisms of aging through three systemic levers.
longevity.pillar1Body: Nutrients are not a simple caloric calculation, but act as vectors of biological information capable of directly modulating gene expression. Through the study of dietary biochemistry and the gut-brain axis, we optimize microbiota activity to silent inflammation (inflammaging) before it causes structural damage to complex tissues.
longevity.pillar2Body: Motor activity administered in adulthood represents an essential pleiotropic drug for welfare and active aging. Muscle contraction responds to precise mechano-biological stimuli and functions as a true endocrine organ, inducing the systemic release of myokines (such as IL-6 and Irisina) capable of reprogramming cellular metabolism and stimulating mitochondrial biogenesis.
longevity.pillar3Body: Chronic stress alters the HPA (hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal) axis and brain circuits, accelerating cellular senescence and organ inflammatory processes. Our protocols target the inflammatory cascade and the polarization of immune cells: by guiding the macrophage switch from the pro-inflammatory M1 phenotype (responsible for fibrosis and decline) to the pro-regenerative M2 phenotype, we preserve neuronal plasticity and accelerate physiological healing.
longevity.social2Text: Precision movement and nutrition are not simple habits; they are integrated biological pharmacology. By studying macrophage polarization (the cellular switch from M1 to M2) and controlling tissue myokines, our research demonstrates that aging can be reprogrammed directly in vivo. The Pillars of Longevity explained through laboratory evidence. 🏃‍♂️🔬
longevity.evidenceTitle: From Academic Validation to Translational Medicine
longevity.evidenceSubtitle: Milestones and protocols of Prof. Ennio Tasciotti in targeting senescence and inflammaging.
longevity.evidenceBio: Consistently ranked in the top 1% of the most influential researchers globally according to Stanford University's scientific ranking and Director of the Human Longevity Program at IRCCS San Raffaele in Rome, Prof. Ennio Tasciotti coordinates the development of nanotechnological solutions and biomaterials applied to health. In 2026, he opened the XXXVIII National Congress of the Italian Sports Medicine Federation (FMSI) presenting the opening Master Lecture dedicated entirely to the engineering of Healthspan.
longevity.paper1Title: Synthetic nanoparticles functionalized with biomimetic leukocyte membranes possess cell-like functions
longevity.paper1Desc: Landmark study on engineering artificial proteo-lipid vesicles (Leuko-Like Vector) mimicking leukocyte membranes to evade immune clearance and selectively target inflamed tissues (Over 1,276 citations).
longevity.paper2Title: Biodegradable silicon nanoneedles delivering nucleic acids intracellularly induce localized in vivo neovascularization
longevity.paper2Desc: Engineering and development of biodegradable porous silicon nanoneedles for localized and controlled delivery of therapeutics and nucleic acids directly into the cellular cytoplasm.
longevity.paper3Title: Rapamycin-loaded biomimetic nanoparticles reverse vascular inflammation
longevity.paper3Desc: Experimental demonstration and validation of reversing chronological vascular inflammatory processes (inflammaging) using biomimetic carriers loaded with rapamycin.
longevity.paper4Title: Mesoporous silicon particles as a multistage delivery system for imaging and therapeutic applications
longevity.paper4Desc: Invention of the Multi Stage Vector delivery system based on porous silicon, recognized by Nature Medicine among the "Five big ideas for nanotechnology" (Over 853 citations).
longevity.patent1Desc: Compositions and structured architectures of bio-inspired trizonal membranes for guided regeneration of complex biological tissues.
longevity.grant1Desc: Scientific direction of a 1 Million Euro research program focused on brain connectivity parameters and anti-inflammatory nanotherapeutics for contrast and early monitoring of Alzheimer's.
longevity.prpDesc: Clinical validation of Platelet-Rich Plasma (PRP) as a biomimetic approach to accelerate early neovascularization and halt biological matrix degradation in degenerative musculoskeletal diseases.
longevity.metric1Lbl: Total citations accumulated in international scientific databases.
longevity.metric2Lbl: Overall H-index (Google Scholar) of Prof. Ennio Tasciotti.
longevity.social3Text: The science of longevity has real value only when founded on rigorous and traceable clinical evidence. From our landmark works in Nature Nanotechnology and Nature Materials on overcoming biological cellular barriers, to the recent 1 million Euro PNRR funding for using nanotherapeutics against inflammaging and neurodegeneration in Alzheimer's disease, our goal remains constant: protecting Healthspan. The biology of aging explained through facts and scientific validation. 🔬🧠

storyAngles.title: Story angles for journalists
items: Chronic inflammation as the master switch of aging: new therapeutic targets.
Personalised nanomedicine: how biomimetic vesicles will transform cancer therapy.
From lab to market: why researchers must become entrepreneurs.

consultancy.title: Consulting
consultancy.subtitle: Top-tier strategic consultant and scientific evaluator for multinational pharmaceutical companies, institutional investment funds, and global innovation ecosystems.

corporate_pharma.title: Strategic Consulting and Corporate Pharma
corporate_pharma.body: Prof. Tasciotti's expertise in applied science and nanomedicine is sought after by global leaders in the biomedical and pharmaceutical industries. Since 2021, he has operated as a Corporate Consultant for the scientific and strategic development of numerous international entities.

investment_funds.title: Advisor for Investment Funds
investment_funds.body: His expertise in the clinical translation of technologies allows him to accurately evaluate the innovation ecosystem for venture capital. He has served as a Consultant and Due Diligence Advisor for leading international institutional funds, providing technical and strategic analysis.

innovation_ecosystems.title: Scientific Guidance for Innovation Ecosystems
innovation_ecosystems.body: Prof. Tasciotti plays a key role as a Scientific Advisor at technology hubs, science parks, and business incubators, promoting technology transfer and serving on numerous prestigious international and governmental committees.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDesc: Full statement on personal data processing in compliance with GDPR.
privacy.intro: This site is operated by Prof. Ennio Tasciotti. We are committed to protecting your personal data in compliance with GDPR (EU) 2016/679.

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.body: Prof. Ennio Tasciotti, IRCCS San Raffaele, Rome, Italy. San Raffaele University, Rome. Email: ennio.tasciotti@sanraffaele.it

data.title: Data We Collect
data.body: We collect only the data you voluntarily provide via the contact or newsletter forms (name, email, message). We do not sell your data. Data is processed via Resend for transactional emails only.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.body: Under GDPR you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict or object to processing of your personal data, and the right to data portability.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.metaDesc: Statement on the use of cookies on enniotasciotti.com.
cookies.intro: This website uses cookies to improve your experience and for analytics purposes.

whatAre.title: What Are Cookies
whatAre.body: Cookies are small text files stored on your device by your browser. They help us understand how visitors use the site.

types.title: Types of Cookies
types.necessary: Necessary cookies: essential for the site to function correctly (e.g. language preference, theme).
types.analytics: Analytics cookies: optional, used to understand site traffic via Google Analytics (with your consent).

manage.title: Managing Cookies
manage.body: You can withdraw consent at any time by clicking the cookie settings button in the site footer. You can also disable cookies via your browser settings.

banner.body: This site uses analytics cookies (Google Analytics). By clicking Accept you consent to their use.
